tasks.title: Create playthrough overlay layer to intercept world-mutating writes
tasks.description: The game currently writes quest status, inventory transfers, and other gameplay changes directly to the canonical world data in MongoDB. This breaks multi-playthrough isolation — if a player completes a quest, it's completed for all future playthroughs. Create a PlaythroughOverlayLayer class (client/src/components/3DGame/PlaythroughOverlayLayer.ts) that intercepts all world-mutating writes and routes them to the playthrough's local state instead. (1) The overlay maintains an in-memory map of entity overrides keyed by entityType+entityId. (2) When the game reads world data (quests, truths, characters, items), the overlay merges base world data with playthrough-local changes. (3) When the game writes (quest completion, inventory transfer, truth creation), the overlay captures the change locally and also records it as a PlaythroughDelta via the existing delta API. (4) The overlay is serialized into the GameSaveState on save and deserialized on load. (5) Both ApiDataSource and FileDataSource route writes through this layer. (6) Add a 'playthroughOverrides' field to the existing GameSaveState interface in shared/game-engine/types.ts. (7) The overlay must handle: quest status changes, new truths created during gameplay, inventory ownership truths, NPC relationship deltas, and XP/level progression. (8) Existing endpoints like PUT /api/quests/:id and POST /api/worlds/:worldId/inventory/transfer should NO LONGER be called from game code — all writes go through the overlay.

tasks.title: Refactor quest state management to use playthrough overlay instead of world writes
tasks.description: Currently, quest completion calls PUT /api/quests/:questId directly from BabylonGame.ts (line ~1792) and QuestCompletionManager.ts (line ~475), which modifies the world quest permanently. Refactor all quest state changes to go through the PlaythroughOverlayLayer. (1) In BabylonGame.ts, replace the PUT /api/quests/:questId call with overlay.updateEntity('quest', questId, { status: 'completed', completedAt: ... }). (2) In QuestCompletionManager.ts, replace the PUT /api/quests/:nextQuestId call for chain activation with overlay.updateEntity('quest', nextQuestId, { status: 'active' }). (3) In DataSource.updateQuest(), route through the overlay instead of hitting the API. (4) When loading quests via DataSource.loadQuests(), merge base world quests with any playthrough overrides from the overlay. (5) The POST /api/worlds/:worldId/quests/:questId/complete endpoint should also be replaced — quest completion scoring and reward application happen locally, and the result is stored in the overlay. (6) Quest chain progression (checking for next quest in chain, activating it) must work entirely from overlayed quest data. (7) Ensure the QuestObjectManager and BabylonQuestTracker read from overlayed data. (8) Add integration test: start playthrough, complete quest, verify world quest is unchanged, verify playthrough overlay has the completion.

tasks.title: Refactor inventory and truth creation to use playthrough overlay
tasks.description: The inventory transfer system (POST /api/worlds/:worldId/inventory/transfer) currently creates, modifies, and deletes Truth entries directly in the world's truth collection. This means item ownership changes persist across all playthroughs. Refactor to use the playthrough overlay. (1) Inventory ownership truths created during gameplay should be stored in the playthrough overlay, not the world truth collection. (2) When loading entity inventory (DataSource.getEntityInventory), merge world-level ownership truths with playthrough-overlay ownership truths. (3) When transferring items, the overlay should: create a new ownership truth for the receiver (in overlay), mark the sender's ownership truth as deleted or modified (in overlay), and create a transaction event truth (in overlay). (4) The existing world truths remain untouched — they represent the 'at game start' state. (5) The FileDataSource.transferItem() stub should now use the same overlay pattern (currently returns { success: true } with no persistence). (6) Gold/currency changes should also be tracked in the overlay rather than modifying character data. (7) Merchant inventory restock/depletion should be overlay-local so merchants reset on new playthrough. (8) Add the overlay's truth additions and deletions to the GameSaveState serialization.

tasks.title: Scope XP, language progress, and CEFR level to playthrough
tasks.description: Currently, XP sync (POST /api/xp/experiences/update) writes to a world-scoped PlayerProgress record, and CEFR level (PUT /api/worlds/:worldId/players/:playerId/language-profile) is stored per-player-per-world with no playthrough awareness. Determine the correct scoping and implement it. (1) Decision point: should language proficiency carry across playthroughs of the same world? For a language learning game, there's a strong argument that the player's real-world language skill persists — but gameplay XP/level may not. Document the decision. (2) If language progress is playthrough-scoped: add playthroughId to the language progress sync payload, vocabulary entries, grammar patterns, and conversation records. Update all telemetry endpoints. (3) If language progress is player-scoped (carries across playthroughs): keep current behavior but ensure XP/gamification state IS playthrough-scoped (so starting a new playthrough resets game progression but not real knowledge). (4) In either case, the LanguageGamificationTracker's XP state (level, totalXP, streaks) should be stored in the playthrough overlay/save state, not a global record. (5) Assessment results (CEFR level) should be associated with the playthrough — a new playthrough should re-assess. (6) Update OnboardingLauncher.ts first-play detection to check per-playthrough, not per-player-per-world. (7) Update the storeCefrLevel function to write to the playthrough record.

tasks.title: Implement playthrough selection and management UI
tasks.description: Players need a UI to create, select, continue, and delete playthroughs before entering a world. Currently, the game auto-starts a single playthrough. (1) Create a PlaythroughSelectScreen component (client/src/components/3DGame/PlaythroughSelectScreen.ts) rendered in Babylon.js GUI. (2) Show a list of the player's existing playthroughs for the current world, each displaying: playthrough name, last played date, play time, current CEFR level, completion percentage. (3) 'New Game' button creates a fresh playthrough (prompts for name). (4) 'Continue' button loads the most recent auto-save for the selected playthrough. (5) 'Delete' button with confirmation dialog removes a playthrough and all its associated data (deltas, traces, saves). (6) For the online/hosted version, fetch playthroughs from GET /api/worlds/:worldId/playthroughs. (7) For exported games, read playthrough list from localStorage/IndexedDB. (8) The screen appears after the world loads but before the game starts. (9) Maximum 5 playthroughs per world per user. (10) Show save slot previews for each playthrough (thumbnail of last save location if available).

tasks.title: Implement save/load game menu with slot management
tasks.description: Create an in-game save/load menu accessible from the game menu (GameMenuSystem.ts). The WorldStateManager already supports 3 save slots and auto-save, but there is no visible UI for manual saves. (1) Add 'Save Game' and 'Load Game' options to the existing game pause menu in GameMenuSystem.ts. (2) Save Game screen shows 3 slots, each displaying: slot name (editable), save date/time, game time played, player location, small screenshot thumbnail if possible. (3) Empty slots show 'Empty Slot — Save Here'. (4) Occupied slots can be overwritten with confirmation. (5) Load Game screen shows the same 3 slots plus the latest auto-save. (6) Loading a save restores all game state via WorldStateManager.load() including the playthrough overlay. (7) Quick-save keybinding (F5) saves to slot 0, quick-load (F9) loads from slot 0. (8) Save/load operations show a brief 'Saving...' / 'Loading...' overlay. (9) For exported games, saves go to localStorage (existing FileDataSource behavior) but scope keys by world ID: insimul_save_{worldId}_{slotIndex}. (10) Warn player if loading will lose unsaved progress.

tasks.title: Implement complete FileDataSource for exported games with local state management
tasks.description: The FileDataSource (client/src/components/3DGame/DataSource.ts) has stub implementations for several write operations that just return { success: true } without actually persisting anything. Flesh these out to work with the PlaythroughOverlayLayer. (1) FileDataSource.transferItem() should update the in-memory overlay and persist to the save state — not just log and return success. (2) FileDataSource.getEntityInventory() should read from the overlay merged with base JSON data. (3) FileDataSource.getMerchantInventory() should generate merchant inventory from base item data + overlay state. (4) FileDataSource.updateQuest() should update the overlay. (5) FileDataSource.payFines() should deduct gold from the overlay's player state. (6) FileDataSource.startPlaythrough() should create a local playthrough record in localStorage/IndexedDB. (7) Add IndexedDB support for larger save states that may exceed localStorage's ~5MB limit. Use localStorage as fallback. (8) Add a migration path: if saves exist under old keys (insimul_save_{slotIndex}), migrate them to new scoped keys (insimul_save_{worldId}_{slotIndex}). (9) FileDataSource should expose the PlaythroughOverlayLayer so game systems can read merged state.

tasks.title: Add playthrough-scoped truth storage and merge logic
tasks.description: Truths are the primary mechanism for recording game events, NPC knowledge, item ownership, and world state changes. Currently all truths are stored in the world's truth collection. Implement playthrough-scoped truth handling. (1) Add a 'playthroughId' optional field to the Truth schema (shared/schema.ts and server/db/mongo-storage.ts). Truths with no playthroughId are 'base' truths from world creation; truths with a playthroughId are gameplay-generated. (2) When loading truths for a playthrough (DataSource.loadTruths), fetch base truths (playthroughId=null) plus playthrough-specific truths (playthroughId=current). (3) Server endpoint: GET /api/worlds/:worldId/truths?playthroughId=X returns merged results. (4) Creating a truth during gameplay should always set the playthroughId. (5) Deleting a truth during gameplay (e.g., consuming an owned item) should mark it as deleted in the overlay rather than actually removing the base truth. (6) For exported games, base truths come from the JSON export; gameplay truths are stored in the save state overlay. (7) When starting a new playthrough, no gameplay truths exist — the player starts with only base truths. (8) NPC dialogue system should read from merged truths so NPCs reference both base world facts and gameplay events.

tasks.title: Add world snapshot versioning and save compatibility checks
tasks.description: The playthrough schema has a worldSnapshotVersion field that is never populated. Implement version tracking so the game can detect when a world has been updated since a save was made. (1) Add a 'version' counter to the World schema that auto-increments whenever the world creator publishes changes (edits characters, quests, settlements, etc.). (2) When starting a playthrough, capture the current world version into playthrough.worldSnapshotVersion. (3) When loading a save, compare the playthrough's snapshot version against the current world version. (4) If versions match, load normally. (5) If the world has been updated, show a warning: 'This world has been updated since your last save. Some content may have changed. Continue anyway?' (6) Implement a basic compatibility check: if the save references quest IDs or character IDs that no longer exist in the world, flag them and handle gracefully (mark quests as abandoned, remove references to deleted characters). (7) For exported games, the world version is baked into the export — saves are always compatible with their export version. (8) Document the versioning scheme so world creators understand the impact of updating a published world.

tasks.title: Implement auto-save triggers and save state completeness audit
tasks.description: The current auto-save runs on a simple 5-minute interval. Enhance it with event-driven triggers and ensure ALL game state is captured. (1) Add auto-save triggers for important events: quest completion, assessment completion, entering/exiting buildings, significant inventory changes (purchase, quest reward), and before the game unloads (beforeunload event). (2) Audit the GameSaveState interface for completeness. Currently missing: language progress (vocabulary, grammar patterns), assessment results, gamification state (XP, level, streaks, achievements), content gating unlocks, NPC schedule states, ambient conversation history, onboarding completion status, world time/date, weather state. (3) Add these to the save state and ensure they're captured/restored. (4) For large save states, implement compression (e.g., JSON → compressed base64) before storing in localStorage or the playthrough.saveData field. (5) Add save state integrity checks: version field, checksum, and graceful handling of corrupted saves. (6) Add a 'last auto-save' indicator in the HUD showing time since last save. (7) The beforeunload handler should use navigator.sendBeacon for reliable persistence. (8) Test save/restore cycle for: mid-conversation save, mid-quest save, save inside building, save during assessment.

tasks.title: Implement NPC relationship and reputation isolation per playthrough
tasks.description: NPC relationships (POST /api/relationships/:char1Id/:char2Id/interact) and salience scores (POST /api/salience/:observerId/:subjectId) currently modify world data directly. Romance data is captured in saves but relationship data may not be fully isolated. (1) NPC-to-player relationships (friendship, trust, romance stage) must be playthrough-scoped. Store in the save state overlay. (2) NPC-to-NPC relationships should start from their world-defined baseline each playthrough, with gameplay changes stored in the overlay. (3) Reputation with settlements is already playthrough-scoped via the playthrough reputation API — verify this works correctly and document it. (4) The relationship endpoints in server/routes.ts should accept an optional playthroughId parameter; when provided, changes are stored in the playthrough's delta layer instead of the world. (5) The WorldStateManager already captures getRelationships() and getRomanceData() — ensure these cover ALL relationship types (not just romance). (6) On save load, restore all relationship states from the overlay. (7) NPC conversation history should be playthrough-scoped — a new playthrough means NPCs don't remember previous conversations. (8) Verify that the BabylonChatPanel's conversation history is included in saves.

tasks.title: Create playthrough data export/import for portable saves
tasks.description: Allow players to export their playthrough data as a portable file and import it elsewhere. This enables save transfer between online and exported versions of the game. (1) Add 'Export Save' button to the playthrough management UI. Generates a .insimul-save file (JSON with metadata + compressed save state + playthrough deltas). (2) The save file format includes: worldId, worldSnapshotVersion, save slot data, playthrough overlay, language progress, assessment results, and a format version number. (3) Add 'Import Save' button that reads a .insimul-save file, validates it against the current world, and creates a new playthrough with the imported state. (4) For online→exported transfer: player exports from the web UI, places the file in the exported game's data/ directory, and the FileDataSource detects and offers to load it. (5) For exported→online transfer: player exports from the game menu, uploads via the web UI. (6) Validate world compatibility on import (worldId must match, warn on version mismatch). (7) Strip any server-specific fields (user IDs, auth tokens) from exports. (8) Size limit: warn if save exceeds 10MB.

tasks.title: Implement offline-first save queue for unreliable connections
tasks.description: For the online/hosted version, saves currently fail silently if the network is unavailable. Implement an offline-first save queue. (1) When ApiDataSource.saveGameState() fails (network error, server error), queue the save in IndexedDB. (2) Show an 'Offline — saves queued locally' indicator in the HUD. (3) When connectivity returns, flush the queue to the server in order. (4) If multiple saves to the same slot are queued, only send the latest. (5) On game load, check for queued saves and flush them before loading game state. (6) Handle conflict: if the server has a newer save than the queued one (e.g., player played on another device), show a conflict resolution dialog. (7) All gameplay should work fully offline using cached world data + local save state. (8) Language progress sync (LanguageProgressTracker) should also queue offline and flush when connected. (9) Assessment submissions should queue similarly. (10) Add a ServiceWorker or background sync registration for flushing when the tab is closed.

tasks.title: Add end-to-end integration tests for the save/load/playthrough system
tasks.description: Create comprehensive integration tests to verify the save system works correctly across all scenarios. (1) Test: create playthrough → play (modify quests, inventory, relationships) → save → reload → verify all state matches. (2) Test: two playthroughs of the same world have independent quest states, inventories, and NPC relationships. (3) Test: world creator modifies world → existing playthrough handles version mismatch gracefully. (4) Test: exported game saves/loads correctly via localStorage with world-scoped keys. (5) Test: save slot management (save to 3 slots, overwrite, delete, load each). (6) Test: auto-save triggers fire at expected events. (7) Test: overlay merge logic produces correct results when base data + overlay have conflicting state. (8) Test: starting a new playthrough gives clean base state with no carryover from previous playthroughs. (9) Test: save file export/import roundtrip preserves all data. (10) Test: offline save queue flushes correctly on reconnection. (11) Use the existing test infrastructure and add fixtures for playthrough scenarios.
